content:
# Philippians Chapter 3 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF page 196 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/PHP_003_page_001.jpg

---

**3:1** The remaining [thing], my brothers, rejoice in [the] Lord. The same things to write to you, to me [is] not tiresome, but for you [is] safe.

**3:2** Look out for the dogs, look out for the evil workers, look out for the mutilation.

**3:3** For we are the circumcision, the [ones] by [the] Spirit of God worshipping and boasting in Christ Jesus and not in flesh having confidence,

**3:4** although I [am] having confidence also in flesh. If any other [man] thinks to have confidence in flesh, I more:

**3:5** circumcision on [the] eighth day, of [the] race of Israel, of [the] tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews, according to [the] law a Pharisee,

**3:6** according to zeal persecuting the assembly, according to righteousness the [one] in [the] law having become blameless.

**3:7** But whatever things were to me gains, these I have counted on account of Christ [as] loss.

**3:8** But indeed also I count all things to be loss on account of the surpassing [thing] of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord, on account of whom the all things I suffered loss, and I count [them] to be refuse, in order that Christ I might gain

**3:9** and might be found in him, not having my righteousness the [one] from [the] law, but the [one] through faith of Christ, the righteousness the [one] from God upon the faith,

**3:10** the to know him and the power of the resurrection of him and the fellowship of the sufferings of him, being conformed to the death of him,

**3:11** if somehow I might attain to the resurrection the [one] from [the] dead.

**3:12** Not that already I received or already have been perfected, but I pursue if also I might lay hold, for which also I was laid hold of by Christ Jesus.

**3:13** Brothers, I myself do not reckon to have laid hold; but one thing, the things behind forgetting and to the things before stretching out,

**3:14** according to [the] goal I pursue toward the prize of the upward calling of God in Christ Jesus.

**3:15** As many therefore [as are] perfect, this let us think; and if anything differently you think, also this God will reveal to you;

**3:16** nevertheless, to what we have attained, by the same to walk.

**3:17** Fellow imitators of me become, brothers, and observe the [ones] thus walking, just as you have us [as] a pattern.

**3:18** For many walk, of whom often I was telling you, and now also weeping I say, the enemies of the cross of Christ,

**3:19** whose the end [is] destruction, whose the god [is] the belly, and the glory in the shame of them, the [ones] the earthly things thinking.

**3:20** For our citizenship in heavens exists, from which also [a] savior we eagerly await, [the] Lord Jesus Christ,

**3:21** who will transform the body of the humiliation of us [to be] conformed to the body of the glory of him, according to the working of the to be able him also to subject to himself the all things.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **3:3** -- Sinaiticus reads ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΙ ΘΕΟΥ (by [the] Spirit of God) for the worship. Some later manuscripts read ΘΕΩ (God) without ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΙ. The Sinaiticus reading ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΙ ΘΕΟΥ λατρευοντες is the earlier and more difficult form.

2. **3:3** -- Sinaiticus reads ΚΑΥΧΩΜΕΝΟΙ (boasting) rather than the variant ΚΑΥΧΩΜΕΝΟΙ ΕΝ -- the preposition ΕΝ (in) before Christ Jesus is present in the manuscript.

3. **3:8** -- Sinaiticus reads ΤΑ ΠΑΝΤΑ ΕΖΗΜΙΩΘΗΝ (the all things I suffered loss). The emphatic ΤΑ ΠΑΝΤΑ placing the article before ΠΑΝΤΑ is characteristic of this manuscript.

4. **3:9** -- Sinaiticus reads ΕΠΙ ΤΗ ΠΙΣΤΕΙ (upon the faith) where many later manuscripts read ΔΙΑ ΠΙΣΤΕΩΣ (through faith). This is a significant variant: Sinaiticus describes righteousness resting "upon" faith rather than coming "through" faith. -- EMET

5. **3:12** -- Sinaiticus reads ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ ΙΗΣΟΥ (Christ Jesus) without the prefix ΥΠΟ in the typical form. The construction ΕΦ Ω ΚΑΙ ΚΑΤΕΛΗΦΘΗΝ (for which also I was laid hold of) is preserved as-is.

6. **3:13** -- Sinaiticus omits ΕΓΩ before ΛΟΓΙΖΟΜΑΙ in certain corrector layers, but the first hand appears to include ΕΜΑΥΤΟΝ (myself) with the verb. The reading "I myself do not reckon" reflects the uncorrected text.

7. **3:16** -- Sinaiticus preserves a shorter reading here: ΤΩ ΑΥΤΩ ΣΤΟΙΧΕΙΝ (by the same to walk). Later Byzantine manuscripts expand this to include ΚΑΝΟΝΙ ΤΟ ΑΥΤΟ ΦΡΟΝΕΙΝ (rule, the same to think). The shorter reading is almost certainly original. -- EMET

8. **3:21** -- Sinaiticus reads ΜΕΤΑΣΧΗΜΑΤΙΣΕΙ (will transform) in the future active indicative, consistent with the eschatological expectation of bodily transformation at Christ's return.

--- EMET
